content.full_text:
M91-019
M

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


December 21, 1990


TO:  Advice File

FROM:  Kevin S. Braaten-Moen

SUBJECT:  When a gift of gubernatorial inaugural ball tickets is
received.

___________________________________________________________________

QUESTIONS

If tickets to the gubernatorial inaugural ball are given to a
legislator at no cost to the legislator, when is the "gift" made?

Does it matter if the tickets are left at the will call window for
the legislator to pick up at the event?

ANSWERS

When the tickets are received by the legislator is the moment at
which the gift is made.  Thus, if the legislator receives the
tickets, in person or by mail, before January 1, 1991, the
legislator will not run afoul of the new gift limit provisions.

If the legislator is told that tickets to the event may be picked
up at the will call window the day of the event, the legislator
receives the gift when s/he picks up the tickets.  Since the event
will take place in 1991, the legislator must take the new gift
limit provisions of the Act into consideration before accepting
the tickets.

If tickets are left at the will call window for the legislator and
the legislator invites someone else to use the tickets instead,
the legislator receives a gift from the giver and the attendee
receives the gift from the legislator when the attendee picks up
the tickets.

This advice concerns the gift limit and reporting provisions of
the Act, not the conflict of interest provisions.

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Advice File Summary
PLEASE FILL IN ALL THE BLANKS!

Type: M                    Staff:    Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Year: 1991                Advisee: ___________________________
No.: 019                  Requestor: ___________________________
Date: 12-21-90            Agency: ___________________________

Subject:        ___ C/I                         ___ C/I Code
                ___ Campaign                     ___ Lobbying
                ___ SEI                          ___ Revolving Door
                ___ Section 84308               ___ Personal Use
                ___ Mass Mail                    _X_ Misc.

Computer Entry Summary: (No more than 5 typed lines for summary.)
Memo to File: a legislator who receives tickets to the gubernatorial inauguration ball before 1/1/91 will not violate the new
gift limits. However, if the legislator picks the tickets up at
the will call window after 1/1/91, the value must be < $250.


Code Section and/or Regulation Nos.

89504


Bulletin Summary: (If you need more space please write on back.)
The new gift limit of $250 from a single source within a calendar
year to a legislator is not in effect for receipt of tickets to
the gubernatorial inauguration ball if the legislator receives the
tickets prior to January 1, 1991. However, if the legislator does
not take possession of the tickets until after January 1, 1991,
(for example, picking the tickets up at the will call window) the
new gift limits are in effect and the gift may not exceed $250.


(Rev. 5/10/90)
